mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in real retail sales signal strengthening consumer demand that lifts corporate revenue expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or the positive return hit rate in months with rising RSAFS is below 55 percent.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate trades below 0.25 percent, the binding zero lower bound forces risk-free rates to the minimum and pushes capital into risk assets, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the following 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are not positive in more than 55 percent of triggered instances when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25 percent, or the t-statistic of mean excess returns is below 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous occurrence of unemployment above 6.5 percent alongside a federal funds rate below 0.25 percent identifies a demand-constrained liquidity trap where earnings growth remains impaired, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of UNRATE above 6.5 percent and FEDFUNDS below 0.25 percent produces a Rank IC below 0.04 or a negative return hit rate below 55 percent over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, month-over-month declines in industrial production signal supply-side bottlenecks and margin compression that precede negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In months classified as late-cycle, declining INDPRO is followed by positive 20-day returns in more than 50 percent of cases, or the Rank IC in that regime falls below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts exceeding 1400 thousand units signal durable goods demand and residential investment strength that spills into broad equity sentiment, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: When HOUST exceeds 1400, the 5-day forward return is positive less than 55 percent of the time, or the mean return is insignificantly different from zero (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in nonfarm payrolls indicates cumulative labor income expansion that lifts aggregate demand with a lag, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC of PAYEMS growth remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive return frequency over 60 days is below 52 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month annualized CPI inflation exceeding 3 percent signals impending monetary tightening pressure that raises near-term equity risk premiums, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: When annualized CPI inflation exceeds 3 percent, 5-day forward returns are negative less than 55 percent of the time, or the Sharpe ratio of the signal is above zero.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter real GDP growth signals aggregate demand expansion that translates into higher corporate earnings over the subsequent quarter, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC of GDPC1 growth remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 60-day return frequency is below 52 percent.
